agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "Parcel delivery logistics platform handling shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment with addresses, transition through states, and classify by weight bracket for billing.",
    "bounded_context": "Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, state lifecycle, weight validation, and weight-bracket classification for pricing.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shipment", "definition": "A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, with a defined weight and lifecycle state." },
      { "term": "Pickup Address", "definition": "The origin location where the parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation." },
      { "term": "Delivery Address", "definition": "The destination location where the parcel must be delivered. Required at shipment creation." },
      { "term": "Shipment State", "definition": "The lifecycle phase of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, In Transit, Delivered. Transitions are strictly forward-only." },
      { "term": "Pending", "definition": "Initial state of a newly created shipment. The shipment exists but has not yet begun physical transport." },
      { "term": "In Transit", "definition": "The shipment is actively being transported. Cannot revert to Pending." },
      { "term": "Delivered", "definition": "Terminal state. The shipment has reached its delivery address. No further state changes are permitted." },
      { "term": "Weight", "definition": "The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive and at most 1000 kg." },
      { "term": "Weight Bracket", "definition": "A pricing classification derived from shipment weight: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (>50 kg)." },
      { "term": "Standard", "definition": "Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg." },
      { "term": "Heavy", "definition": "Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg." },
      { "term": "Freight", "definition": "Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment without both endpoints is logically meaningless — it cannot represent a physical delivery. This is a data integrity constraint; the entity is corrupt without it.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create a shipment with a null or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Shipment state transitions are strictly forward-only: Pending → In Transit → Delivered.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Backward transitions would corrupt the shipment lifecycle and break audit integrity. A parcel that was physically in transit cannot logically revert to pending.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment in state 'In Transit' cannot transition to 'Pending'. A shipment in state 'Delivered' cannot transition to any other state."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Delivered is a terminal state — no further state changes are permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Delivery finality is a core domain truth. Re-opening a delivered shipment would corrupt billing, tracking records, and customer notifications. The physical act of delivery is irreversible.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected, regardless of the target state."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would corrupt weight-bracket classification and billing calculations.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected at creation and at any weight update."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 1000 kg cap is a business policy reflecting current fleet and operational capacity. A VP could raise this limit if the company acquires heavier-capacity vehicles. It is not a physical law.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Weight bracket classification: Standard for weight in (0, 10] kg, Heavy for weight in (10, 50] kg, Freight for weight in (50, 1000] kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Bracket boundaries are a pricing policy decision. Thresholds (10 kg, 50 kg) could be adjusted by business leadership without corrupting system integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "A 10 kg shipment is classified as Standard. A 10.01 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50.01 kg shipment is classified as Freight."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "A newly created shipment starts in the Pending state.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment cannot be created directly as In Transit or Delivered — that would skip required lifecycle phases and corrupt tracking and audit history.",
      "testable_assertion": "After successful creation, a shipment's state must be Pending."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A valid pickup address, delivery address, and weight of 5 kg",
      "when": "A shipment is created",
      "then": "The shipment is persisted in Pending state with weight bracket Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A creation request missing the pickup address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating pickup address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A creation request missing the delivery address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating delivery address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to In Transit",
      "then": "The state changes to In Transit and the transition is recorded"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending",
      "then": "The transition is rejected and the shipment remains In Transit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to Delivered",
      "then": "The state changes to Delivered and the transition is recorded as final"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A shipment in Delivered state",
      "when": "Any state change is attempted (to Pending, In Transit, or Delivered)",
      "then": "The transition is rejected and the shipment remains Delivered"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A shipment creation request with weight of 0 kg",
      "when": "The shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A shipment creation request with weight of -5 kg",
      "when": "The shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A shipment creation request with weight of 1001 kg",
      "when": "The shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight exceeds maximum of 1000 kg"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 10 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of 10.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 50 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-14",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of 50.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Freight"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-15",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 1000 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Freight and creation succeeds"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Weight at exact boundary of 10 kg", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Standard (upper-inclusive). The spec says '0-10 kg is standard', implying 10 kg is included in Standard." },
    { "scenario": "Weight at exact boundary of 50 kg", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Heavy (upper-inclusive). The spec says '10-50 kg is heavy', implying 50 kg is included in Heavy." },
    { "scenario": "Weight of exactly 1000 kg", "expected_behavior": "Accepted and classified as Freight. 1000 kg is the upper bound, not exceeded." },
    { "scenario": "Attempt to skip Pending and create directly as In Transit", "expected_behavior": "Rejected. All shipments must start as Pending per the lifecycle invariant." },
    { "scenario": "Attempt to transition Pending directly to Delivered (skipping In Transit)", "expected_behavior": "Ambiguous — spec does not explicitly allow or disallow this. Recommend rejecting to enforce full lifecycle sequence." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup and delivery addresses are identical", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address this. May be valid in edge cases (e.g., internal warehouse moves) or may indicate a data entry error. Needs business clarification." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is a very small positive number (e.g., 0.001 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Should be accepted — spec only requires weight > 0 and <= 1000 kg. Classified as Standard." },
    { "scenario": "Attempt to modify weight after shipment is In Transit or Delivered", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address mutability of weight post-creation. Weight changes after transit begins could affect billing integrity — needs business clarification." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Weight bracket classification directly drives pricing and revenue. Incorrect bracket assignment means under- or over-charging customers. The 1000 kg cap defines the addressable market ceiling for the platform.",
    "operational": "State machine correctness ensures reliable tracking, dispatch coordination, and delivery confirmation workflows. Violations would create phantom shipments, duplicated deliveries, or lost parcels in operational dashboards.",
    "user": "Customers rely on state transitions for delivery tracking. Terminal Delivered state gives confidence that tracking is authoritative. Clear validation errors at creation time prevent frustration from silently rejected or malformed shipments.",
    "risk": "Primary risks: (1) billing disputes from incorrect weight bracket classification at boundaries, (2) data corruption from backward state transitions, (3) operational chaos if Delivered shipments can be reopened. All are mitigated by the invariants defined above.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Shippers (customers)", "impact": "Depend on correct weight bracket for accurate pricing quotes and on state transitions for shipment visibility." },
      { "group": "Dispatch / Operations", "impact": "Rely on forward-only state machine to coordinate pickups, routing, and delivery confirmation." },
      { "group": "Finance / Billing", "impact": "Weight brackets map directly to pricing tiers; boundary errors cause revenue leakage or customer disputes." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Terminal Delivered state reduces dispute volume. Clear validation errors reduce support tickets for failed creation attempts." },
      { "group": "Drivers / Couriers", "impact": "Depend on In Transit state accuracy to know which shipments are assigned and active." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec is clear on core lifecycle and weight rules but leaves several boundary and policy questions unresolved: exact bracket boundary inclusivity (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?), whether Pending→Delivered skip is allowed, address validation rules, and weight mutability post-creation. These are common sources of implementation bugs and need PM clarification for a production system.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Forward-only state machine (Pending → In Transit → Delivered)",
      "Delivered is terminal and irreversible",
      "Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg",
      "Both addresses required at creation",
      "Three weight brackets exist with the described ranges"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Exact inclusivity of weight bracket boundaries (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?)",
      "Whether Pending can skip directly to Delivered",
      "Whether pickup and delivery address can be identical",
      "Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation",
      "What constitutes a valid address (format, completeness)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Weight brackets use upper-inclusive boundaries based on natural reading of '0-10 kg is standard'",
      "Shipments always start in Pending state — there is no direct creation into other states",
      "The state machine requires sequential transitions (Pending→In Transit→Delivered, no skipping)",
      "Weight is immutable after creation (conservative assumption for billing integrity)"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Weight bracket boundary inclusivity is not explicit. The spec says '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy' — does a 10 kg shipment fall into Standard or Heavy? Both brackets reference 10 kg.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Shippers"],
      "options": ["10 kg is Standard (upper-inclusive for lower bracket)", "10 kg is Heavy (lower-inclusive for upper bracket)"],
      "recommendation": "10 kg as Standard (upper-inclusive) is the more natural reading and more customer-friendly. Confirm with PM."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Can a shipment transition directly from Pending to Delivered, skipping In Transit? The spec only explicitly forbids In Transit → Pending and any change from Delivered.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Dispatch / Operations", "Drivers / Couriers"],
      "options": ["Allow Pending → Delivered (for edge cases like immediate local delivery)", "Require Pending → In Transit → Delivered (full lifecycle enforcement)"],
      "recommendation": "Require full sequential lifecycle (Pending → In Transit → Delivered) to maintain operational integrity and audit trail. Skipping In Transit loses transport tracking data."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No rules specified for address validation — what constitutes a valid pickup or delivery address? No mention of whether addresses can be identical or what fields are required.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Shippers", "Dispatch / Operations", "Drivers / Couriers"],
      "options": ["Accept any non-empty string", "Require structured address with street, city, postal code, country", "Integrate with geocoding/address verification service"],
      "recommendation": "At minimum require non-empty strings. Structured validation rules should be defined by PM based on operational geography."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
